This is an enterprise Java portal application built on the Axon Ivy Platform (a workflow/process management platform).

Technology Stack:
- Java (primary language)
- XHTML Facelets templates (JSF/PrimeFaces UI)
- Axon Ivy Process definitions (.p files)
- Maven build system
- REST APIs
- JavaScript (client-side)

Focus Areas for This Project:

1. INJECTION ATTACKS (Critical):
   - SQL injection in database queries (especially dynamic JPQL/HQL)
   - EL expression injection in XHTML Facelets templates
   - Command injection via Runtime.exec() or ProcessBuilder
   - LDAP injection in user/role lookups
   - XPath injection in XML processing

2. CROSS-SITE SCRIPTING (Critical):
   - Stored XSS via user-supplied content rendered in XHTML templates
   - Reflected XSS in URL parameters used in process start links
   - DOM-based XSS in JavaScript code
   - Unescaped output in PrimeFaces components

3. AUTHENTICATION & AUTHORIZATION (Critical):
   - Broken access control in Ivy REST endpoints
   - Privilege escalation through task delegation/reassignment
   - Missing permission checks on process starts
   - Session fixation or session management issues

4. SENSITIVE DATA EXPOSURE (High):
   - Hardcoded credentials, API keys, or secrets
   - Sensitive data in log output
   - Information disclosure in error messages
   - PII handling violations

5. INSECURE DESERIALIZATION (High):
   - Unsafe Java object deserialization
   - Unvalidated JSON/XML deserialization

6. XML EXTERNAL ENTITY (High):
   - XXE in XML parsers processing user-supplied XML
   - XXE in process definition parsing

7. SERVER-SIDE REQUEST FORGERY (Medium):
   - SSRF via unvalidated URLs in REST client calls
   - SSRF in webhook or notification URLs

8. PATH TRAVERSAL (Medium):
   - File upload/download without path sanitization
   - Document management file access issues

Exclude from scanning:
- Test files (*Test.java, *-test/ directories)
- Documentation files (*.rst, *.md in Documentation/)
- Build scripts and CI/CD configurations
- Generated code in target/ directories
- Third-party library code

Axon Ivy Specific Security Concerns:
- Process permissions: Verify role-based access control in .p process files
- CMS entries: Check for unsanitized HTML content injection
- Ivy scripting: Look for unsafe expressions in process scripts
- REST service security: Validate authentication on custom REST services
- SubProcess calls: Check for parameter injection between processes
